evidence_basis_summary: Both sources fix the evidence universe a priori (ANES 2016/2020 waves in P1; 210 TESS studies in S1); no human adjudication of inclusion is described or contested.
notes: Upgraded: full-text confirms benchmark sets are prefixed by design; not applicable is defensible from direct reading.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 constrains acceptable causal/regression interpretation of LLM outputs (48% of coefficients differ, sign flips 32%) and warns against inferring mechanisms; S1 finetuning focuses on prediction not mechanism.
notes: Upgraded from low to medium: P1 Section 2.2 provides indirect but clear pressure on mechanism claims from synthetic data.

evidence_basis_summary: Feeling-thermometer construct and target-group list are inherited from ANES instrument (P1); S1 inherits TESS outcome scales; humans do not renegotiate construct boundaries in either case.
notes: Upgraded: direct text shows constructs are borrowed from external instruments rather than set by the research team.

evidence_basis_summary: Evidence base is a single canonical survey (ANES) in P1 and a single repository (TESS) in S1; neither case foregrounds corpus pluralism.
notes: Upgraded: sources are explicit about relying on one canonical benchmark each.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 Discussion explicitly calls for preregistering prompts, archiving historical LLMs, and new reproducibility standards after documenting April/June/July drift and prompt sensitivity; SI details prompt-versioning workflow.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: reproducibility/provenance work is a central contribution of P1 (Section 3.2 and Discussion).

evidence_basis_summary: P1 SI Sections 12-13 and Discussion directly assess whether prompting practices generalize to other surveys/countries and conclude transportability is poor; S1 evaluates generalization to unseen studies, conditions, outcomes, participants.
notes: Evidence directly concerns this role in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: Both studies are API-mediated persona simulations with no fieldwork or interview presence; the absence of situated interaction is itself the object of critique in P1.
notes: Upgraded: not applicable is directly adjudicable from the methods.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 Discussion raises ethics of replacing human respondents with synthetic ones and flags unknown-assumption accountability gaps; author-contribution statement assigns task-level responsibility.
notes: Upgraded: P1's closing ethics paragraph gives indirect but explicit pressure on who is answerable for synthetic-opinion outputs.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither source contests category definitions; feeling-thermometer groups and TESS outcomes are taken as given.
notes: Upgraded: construct boundaries are inherited rather than set.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 shows synthetic data flatten subgroup variance and exaggerate out-group antipathy among Democrats/Republicans; S1 measures demographic parity difference and reports 10.6% bias reduction via finetuning.
notes: Upgraded from no/low to partial/medium: preserving subgroup disagreement is explicitly at stake (Figures 1-2 in P1; section 5.6 in S1). Observability changed to partial.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 SI 12-13 documents degraded performance in non-ANES and non-US contexts; S1 sections 5.3-5.5 formalizes generalization to unseen studies, conditions, outcomes, and participants.
notes: Upgraded from low to high: both sources directly operationalize transportability tests.

evidence_basis_summary: P1 benchmarks ChatGPT means/variances/regression coefficients against ANES as reference distribution; S1 explicitly frames the task as distributional alignment via Wasserstein distance with Empirical-Best and Uniform-Guess bounds.
notes: Core role for this case; evidence directly on point in both sources.

evidence_basis_summary: No interviewer or fieldworker is present; both studies operate via API-prompted personas rather than live interaction.
notes: Upgraded: methods sections make the absence explicit.

evidence_basis_summary: No archival or tacit contextual interpretation is performed; synthetic outputs are scored against numeric survey benchmarks.
Neither case involves title/abstract triage or study screening; S1's agent parses TESS data into a uniform format but this is data reconstruction, not screening.
notes: Upgraded: task type is absent from both workflows.

role_label: Routine coder on high-consensus, ground-truth-rich tasks
evidence_basis_summary: S1 reframes finetuned LLMs as auditable predictors of individual responses with ground-truth TESS labels (accuracy + Wasserstein); P1 shows that without ground truth, routine delegation is unsafe.
notes: Upgraded from no/low to partial/medium: S1's SOCRATES finetuning is a clear instance of routinizable prediction on ground-truth-rich survey data, while P1 is the contrary case warning against premature delegation. Observability changed to partial.

evidence_basis_summary: Neither case concerns sentence-level drafting or prose revision; the LLM is queried for numeric feeling-thermometer or ordinal survey responses, not prose output.
notes: Upgraded: adjudicable directly from the elicitation format.
